name: Stats I/O and merge
summary: Complete stats merge/YAML and rust-htslib BGZF threaded I/O behavior without runtime pairtools shellouts.
goal: Implement oracle-tested `pairs-rs stats --merge`, YAML output for stats reports, and supported `--nproc-in/--nproc-out` BGZF threading through HTSlib.
explicit_non_goals: Do not implement split, restrict, phase, sample, scaling, filterbycov, parse2, or header behavior.
Do not call Python pairtools at Rust runtime.
Do not implement `--cmd-in` or `--cmd-out` by shelling out in Rust runtime.
Do not benchmark or claim speedup.
acceptance_criteria: `pairs-rs stats --merge` matches pairtools merge behavior for committed small TSV stats fixtures.
`pairs-rs stats --yaml` matches pairtools YAML for committed small fixtures or documents exact scoped parity.
`--nproc-in` and `--nproc-out` control HTSlib BGZF threading for `.gz` stats input/output.
`--cmd-in` and `--cmd-out` fail loudly instead of shelling out.
known_risks: Pairtools YAML formatting and stats merge behavior must be kept deterministic without adding Python runtime dependencies.
